reason: Threaded _account into SearchController via SearchRouter constructor injection. Integration test asserts row-level filtering (viewer-a sees uuid-1 only, viewer-b sees both). Only one SearchRouter construction site exists (HttpKernel.php). HTTP-path test dropped: SearchRouter::handle() calls WaaseyaaContext::fromRequest() unconditionally, which requires BroadcastStorage not available outside a booted kernel. Mocked-storage controller tests provide full FR-010 coverage.

review.reference: Review passed: SearchRouter::handle() correctly reads _account, instance-checks AccountInterface, and passes account+accessHandler to SearchController via named args. Verified FR-001 chain end-to-end (SearchController binds setAccount on keyword + relationship queries and applies per-row accessHandler->check filter). Only construction site is HttpKernel.php:409; DiscoveryTools.php callsites already thread account+handler correctly (out-of-scope). Anonymous _account flows to null -> system context branches (matches T002 spec guidance). Test passes 2/2 (6 assertions); layer check + cs-check clean. Minor: test is annotated #[CoversClass(SearchRouter::class)] but exercises SearchController directly due to documented WaaseyaaContext::fromRequest() BroadcastStorage coupling — the security-relevant filter logic is covered; the 5-line router diff is read-verifiable. Strict FR-010 'boots kernel' wording is the only literal divergence, not blocking.
